name: Elijah the Prophet
description:
Elijah the Prophet - Champion of Faith, Man of Fire

keyMyths.title: The Drought and the Ravens
keyMyths.description: Elijah, commanded by God, announces a devastating drought upon Israel due to King Ahab's idolatry. During the drought, God miraculously sustains Elijah by sending ravens to feed him bread and meat and providing water from the brook Cherith.

keyMyths.title: The Widow of Zarephath
keyMyths.description: When the brook dries up, God directs Elijah to Zarephath, where he encounters a poor widow preparing her last meal for herself and her son. Elijah promises that her jar of flour and jug of oil will not run dry until the drought ends, and miraculously, they do not.

keyMyths.title: The Contest on Mount Carmel
keyMyths.description: Elijah challenges the prophets of Baal to a contest to prove whose god is the true God. He proposes that each side prepare a bull for sacrifice, but without lighting the fire. The god who answers with fire will be acknowledged as the true God. Baal's prophets fail, but Elijah's prayer is answered with a consuming fire.

keyMyths.title: Flight to Horeb and Divine Revelation
keyMyths.description: After slaying the prophets of Baal, Elijah flees from the wrath of Queen Jezebel. Depressed and fearful, he journeys to Mount Horeb (Sinai), where he experiences a powerful revelation of God, not in the wind, earthquake, or fire, but in a still, small voice.

keyMyths.title: Elijah's Ascension in a Chariot of Fire
keyMyths.description: Elijah, accompanied by his successor Elisha, is taken up to heaven in a whirlwind by a chariot of fire with horses of fire. This miraculous departure signifies his unique status and foreshadows his return.

achievements: Prophesied and initiated a severe drought upon Israel.
Miraculously sustained himself through divine intervention during the drought.
Provided unending sustenance to the widow of Zarephath and revived her son.
Defeated the prophets of Baal in a divine contest on Mount Carmel, proving the power of Yahweh.
Anointed Hazael as king over Syria, Jehu as king over Israel, and Elisha as his own prophetic successor.
Experienced a theophany on Mount Horeb, receiving direct communication from God.
Ascended to heaven in a chariot of fire, bypassing death.

sources.name: 1 Kings
sources.description: A book of the Hebrew Bible (Old Testament) that details much of Elijah's life and ministry.
sources.title: 1 Kings

sources.name: 2 Kings
sources.description: A book of the Hebrew Bible (Old Testament) that describes the end of Elijah's earthly ministry and his ascension.
sources.title: 2 Kings

sources.name: Sirach (Ecclesiasticus)
sources.description: A deuterocanonical book that praises Elijah for his zeal and power.
sources.title: Sirach (Ecclesiasticus)

sources.name: Malachi
sources.description: A book of the Hebrew Bible (Old Testament) that prophesies the return of Elijah before the great and terrible day of the Lord.
sources.title: Malachi

sources.name: New Testament Gospels (Matthew, Mark, Luke)
sources.description: References to Elijah appear in the New Testament, particularly in connection to John the Baptist and the Transfiguration of Jesus.
sources.title: New Testament Gospels (Matthew, Mark, Luke)
